name: Comprehensive Local Care Expansion Pack
description: Supplemental local coverage for home vitals, specialist follow-up, medication safety, lab interpretation, wellness planning, records, insurance readiness, and procedures.
reviewStatus: local pack requires clinician, pharmacy, and workflow review before production medical use

records.title: Home Blood Pressure Technique Audit
records.summary: Organizes home blood pressure review using cuff size, seated rest, arm positioning, repeat checks, caffeine, stress, and symptom context before judging a reading.
records.safetyNotes: Very high BP with chest pain, severe headache, neurologic symptoms, or shortness of breath needs direct medical review rather than technique troubleshooting.
whatToTrack: Cuff size, arm used, seated rest time, feet position, and whether talking or movement happened during the reading.
Two or three repeat readings with time gaps, symptoms, and medicine timing.
Recent caffeine, nicotine, exercise, pain, illness, or stress exposure.
careQuestions: What makes a home BP reading more reliable before I compare it with previous readings?
Which setup mistakes can make a single reading look higher or lower than usual?
precautions: Do not dismiss severe symptoms because a repeat reading improves slightly.
Do not adjust BP medicines from one home reading without clinician direction.

records.title: Standing Dizziness, Hydration, and Orthostatic Review
records.summary: Builds a structured review for dizziness when standing up, getting out of bed, or rising from a chair using hydration, illness, diarrhea, fever, medicines, pulse, blood pressure, and fainting risk.
records.safetyNotes: Fainting, chest pain, confusion, severe weakness, black stool, or major ongoing vomiting needs direct medical assessment.

records.title: Glucose Pattern, Meals, and Sick-Day Review
records.summary: Reviews glucose patterns around meals, illness, dehydration, missed medicines, sleep, and activity while preparing questions for diabetes follow-up.
records.safetyNotes: Confusion, repeated vomiting, inability to keep fluids down, very low sugar symptoms, or ketone concerns need real-world medical review.

records.title: Heart Failure Daily Weight and Fluid Review
records.summary: Organizes heart-failure follow-up using daily weight change, swelling, breathing at rest, pillows needed at night, urine response, and medicine timing.
records.safetyNotes: Rapid breathing worsening, chest pain, fainting, confusion, blue lips, or sudden major swelling should be treated as urgent medical needs.

records.title: COPD Flare, Oxygen, and Trigger Review
records.summary: Structures COPD or chronic lung follow-up using oxygen wording, sputum change, wheeze, inhaler use, exertion limits, smoke exposure, and rescue plan questions.
records.safetyNotes: Blue lips, severe breathing work, confusion, chest pain, or inability to speak in full sentences needs urgent care.

records.title: Anemia, Ferritin, and Iron Study Review
records.summary: Explains anemia and iron-study terms using fatigue, bleeding history, diet, pregnancy context, prior results, and clinician-ready follow-up questions.
records.safetyNotes: Chest pain, fainting, black stool, severe weakness, or rapidly worsening shortness of breath needs prompt medical evaluation.

records.title: Vitamin D, B12, and Nutrition Lab Review
records.summary: Organizes low vitamin or nutrition-result questions using symptoms, supplements, food pattern, stomach issues, repeat testing, and clinician follow-up planning.
records.safetyNotes: New weakness, confusion, severe numbness, or rapidly worsening symptoms should not wait for routine vitamin discussion.

records.title: Contrast Imaging Kidney and Allergy Checklist
records.summary: Prepares questions before contrast imaging using kidney labs, prior reactions, hydration, diabetes medicines, pregnancy, and same-day instructions.
records.safetyNotes: Breathing trouble, swelling, or prior severe contrast reaction history should be escalated directly to the imaging team or clinician.

records.title: Colonoscopy Prep, Recovery, and Follow-up Checklist
records.summary: Builds a colonoscopy preparation and recovery checklist using bowel prep instructions, hydration, escort planning, blood-thinner questions, and post-procedure warning signs.
records.safetyNotes: Heavy bleeding, severe abdominal pain, fever, chest symptoms, or fainting after a procedure needs direct medical review.

records.title: Statin Muscle, Liver, and Interaction Safety Review
records.summary: Organizes statin-safety questions using muscle symptoms, weakness timing, dark urine, liver-test wording, grapefruit, and interaction context.
records.safetyNotes: Severe muscle weakness, dark urine, major abdominal symptoms, or fainting needs clinician or urgent review instead of routine app advice.

records.title: Controller versus Rescue Inhaler Review
records.summary: Explains controller and rescue inhaler roles, technique questions, spacer use, rinse instructions, refill readiness, and warning-sign boundaries.
records.safetyNotes: Severe breathing trouble or frequent rescue inhaler dependence with poor relief needs prompt real-world clinical assessment.

records.title: Steroid Course, Glucose, and Infection Precautions
records.summary: Builds a steroid-course review using duration, glucose changes, swelling, sleep or mood effects, infection exposure, and follow-up questions.
records.safetyNotes: Severe mood change, breathing problems, black stool, confusion, or major glucose symptoms needs direct medical review.

records.title: Panic Symptoms versus Medical Red-Flag Sorting Guide
records.summary: Helps separate anxiety-style symptom context from medical red flags by organizing onset, trigger, duration, prior history, breathing pattern, chest symptoms, and fainting risk.
records.safetyNotes: New chest pain, fainting, severe breathlessness, neurologic symptoms, or persistent unexplained symptoms should not be dismissed as anxiety.

records.title: Shift Work, Sleep Recovery, and Fatigue Plan
records.summary: Creates a practical wellness plan for shift work using sleep timing, light exposure, caffeine window, hydration, meals, and safe fatigue-management questions.
records.safetyNotes: Severe daytime sleepiness affecting driving or major breathing symptoms during sleep should be escalated to real-world care.

records.title: Family History and Screening Organizer
records.summary: Organizes family-history discussions around condition type, age at diagnosis, screening already done, smoking, weight, and the next clinician question.
records.safetyNotes: This is a screening-preparation tool. Final screening decisions should be confirmed with a clinician.

records.title: Referral, Network, and Benefit Check Workflow
records.summary: Builds an insurance workflow for checking referrals, in-network status, precertification, member services notes, estimated cost sharing, and next follow-up steps.
records.safetyNotes: This supports benefits organization only and does not confirm live insurer decisions or legal coverage outcomes.

records.title: Symptom Diary, Trigger, and Progression Record
records.summary: Creates a local symptom-diary structure using onset, trigger, timing, severity, what changed, readings, medicines, and care-team questions.
records.safetyNotes: A diary should not delay urgent care when symptoms are severe, sudden, or rapidly worsening.

records.title: Medication Adherence Barrier Review
records.summary: Organizes adherence barriers using refill delays, cost, side effects, schedule complexity, storage, travel, and reminder tools before clinician or pharmacist follow-up.
records.safetyNotes: Do not stop important long-term medicines because of barriers without contacting a clinician or pharmacist when needed.
